meta.title: Greenways site knowledge cards
meta.description: Grounded claims agents may cite in chat — numbers must match linked JSON or official pages. Additive only; never replaces existing answers.

cards.proseTemplate: For example, our **{title}** on Greenways shows running cost around **€{baselineMonthlyEur}/mo → €{proposedMonthlyEur}/mo**, capex **~€{capexEur}**, with an illustrative **€{grantEur}** grant on the chart — open the projection module for payback timing.

Our **{title}** scenario models **€{baselineMonthlyEur}/mo → €{proposedMonthlyEur}/mo** with capex **~€{capexEur}** and an example **€{grantEur}** grant — useful when warewash load drives peak water and heat.

On the **wok line** worked example, Greenways shows **€{baselineMonthlyEur}/mo → €{proposedMonthlyEur}/mo** gas-equivalent duty with **~€{capexEur}** capex and **€{grantEur}** illustrative grant — {savingsNote}.
Cold chain upgrades often start with numbers: our **{title}** chart uses **€{baselineMonthlyEur}/mo → €{proposedMonthlyEur}/mo**, **~€{capexEur}** capex, **€{grantEur}** example grant.

Kitchen **HVAC heat recovery** on our projection page illustrates **€{baselineMonthlyEur}/mo → €{proposedMonthlyEur}/mo** with **~€{capexEur}** capex and **€{grantEur}** example grant — fan and gas heating offset combined.

cards.prose: On Greenways, **etl_*** rows mirror the UK **Energy Technology List** — products are independently verified and typically in the **top 25%** of their class, so procurement risk is lower than brochure claims alone. Open the official ETL module to read the scheme in DESNZ's own words.

A practical ETL path on Greenways: **official register** → **ETL finder** shortlist → **equipment deep dive** side-by-side → **savings projection** for payback → **Andrieus** for grants and **Vincent** for finance.

Before heavy kitchen capex, **measuring use** often pays back fast: published office **aM&T** programmes commonly find **4–20%** savings (**~10–15%** typical), and tenant sub-metering in housing often lands **10–15%** when bills reflect real use — our monitoring guide walks through methods and European case studies.

Fabric first: lowering heating and cooling baseload with **insulation** and ventilation discipline often makes cookline upgrades smaller and payback faster — our renovation and insulation modules order fabric, HVAC, and equipment swaps sensibly.

Typical finance stack on Greenways: model payback in **savings projection**, check non-repayable support in the **finance finder grants** tab or **Andrieus**, then match **BNPL**, **equipment finance**, or **green loans** to your capex band — tariffs and ticker context explain why efficient kit matters when €/kWh moves.
